2603.20686.title: SNAP: Speaker Nulling for Artifact Projection in Speech Deepfake Detection
2603.20686.paper_summary: Recent advancements in text-to-speech technologies enable generating high-fidelity synthetic speech nearly indistinguishable from real human voices. While recent studies show the efficacy of self-supervised learning-based speech encoders for deepfake detection, these models struggle to generalize across unseen speakers. Our quantitative analysis suggests these encoder representations are substantially influenced by speaker information, causing detectors to exploit speaker-specific correlations rather than artifact-related cues. We call this phenomenon speaker entanglement. To mitigate this reliance, we introduce SNAP, a speaker-nulling framework. We estimate a speaker subspace and apply orthogonal projection to suppress speaker-dependent components, isolating synthesis artifacts within the residual features. By reducing speaker entanglement, SNAP encourages detectors to focus on artifact-related patterns, leading to state-of-the-art performance.
2603.20686.paper_summary_zh: 最近在文本轉語音技術方面的進展使得生成的合成語音幾乎無法與真實人聲區分開來。<lb/>雖然最近的研究顯示基於自我監督學習的語音編碼器在深偽檢測方面的有效性，但這些模型在未見過的說話者之間的泛化能力較弱。<lb/>我們的定量分析表明，這些編碼器表示受到說話者信息的顯著影響，導致檢測器利用說話者特定的相關性，而非與工件相關的線索。<lb/>我們稱這一現象為說話者纏結。<lb/>為了減少這種依賴，我們引入了SNAP，一個說話者消除框架。<lb/>我們估計一個說話者子空間並應用正交投影來抑制依賴說話者的組件，從而在殘差特徵中隔離合成工件。<lb/>通過減少說話者纏結，SNAP促使檢測器專注於與工件相關的模式，從而實現了最先進的性能。
2603.20686.author: Kyudan Jung et.al.
2603.20686.authors: Kyudan Jung, Jihwan Kim, Minwoo Lee, Soyoon Kim, Jeonghoon Kim, Jaegul Choo, Cheonbok Park
